file.content:
---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, and run commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain
- Path: `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`
- Compiler: `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 14.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)

## QEMU (Andes fork)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)
- For VLEN=1024: change to `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` in run.sh line ~101
- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)

## Running a Single Test via QEMU

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 path/to/test.adx
```

content.text: Let me check the run script and sim wrapper to understand the FPGA flow.

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→################################################################################
     3→# Input:
     4→#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
     5→################################################################################
     6→CWD=$(pwd)
     7→
     8→#----- set up the tool name -----
     9→source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64
    10→
    11→#----- build and run switches -----
    12→ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
    13→ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
    14→ENA_RUN_GRU=0
    15→ENA_RUN_PNET=0
    16→ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
    17→ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
    18→ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
    19→ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
    20→ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
    21→RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
    22→ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
    23→ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
    24→ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
    25→ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
    26→ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
    27→ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
    28→ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
    29→ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
    30→ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
    31→ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
    32→VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d
    33→
    34→#----- flags of library and app -----
    35→CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
    36→CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
    37→APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
    38→LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
    39→if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    40→    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
    41→        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    42→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    43→        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    44→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    45→        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    46→    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
    47→        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    48→    else
    49→        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    50→    fi

    54→    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"

    58→fi

    61→fi

    85→    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"

   111→    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"

   131→if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then

#!/usr/bin/env bash
################################################################################
# Input:
#  $1: extra compilation flags or macro for both libray and application
################################################################################
CWD=$(pwd)

#----- set up the tool name -----
source ../NLIB_CROSS_V5_RV64

#----- build and run switches -----
ENA_BUILD_LIBNN=1
ENA_RUN_CIFAR10=0
ENA_RUN_GRU=0
ENA_RUN_PNET=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN=0
ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW=0
ENA_RUN_DW_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST=0
ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC=1
RUN_CONFIG=2        #0: run app on board, 1: run app on SID, 2: run app on QEMU
ENA_CLANG=0         #0: use gcc as compiler, 1: use clang as compiler
ENA_RUN_TWICE=0     #0: run function once, 1: run function twice
ENA_VEC_ISA=1       #0: disable v-ext (for measuring plain C algo performance), 1: enable v-ext
ENA_VDOT=1          #0: use the default v-ext algorithm, 1: use vdot v-ext algorithm
ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0    #0: using the inline assembly version v-ext algorithm, 1: use the intrinsic version v-ext algorithm
ENA_HVM=0           #0: place the program's data on cache, 1: place the program's data on HVM
ENA_HPM=0           #0: disable the performance monitor, 1: enable the performance monitor
ENA_ACE_RVV=0       #0: disabe the ACE-RVV, 1: enable the ACE-RVV
ENA_ZVLSSEG=0       #0: disable zvlsseg extension, 1: enable zvlsseg extension
ENA_VL4=1           #0: disable VL4 extension, 1: enable VL4 extension
VEC_CONFIG=4        #0: zve32x, 1: zve32f, 2: zve64x, 3: zve64f, 4: zv64d

#----- flags of library and app -----
CPU_NAME="ax45mpv"
CPU_FLAG="-mtune=andes-45-series"
APP_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./../../Examples/unit_func/include -I./../../Include -I./../../internal"
LIB_INCLUDE_PATH="-I./Include -I./internal"
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]]; then
    if [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64x"
    elif [[ ${VEC_CONFIG} -eq 3 ]]; then
        VEC_CFG="zve64f"
    else
        VEC_CFG="zve64d"
    fi

    CPU_FLAG+=" -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
    # export the VEC_CFG environment variable for selecting the proper QEMU conf
    export VEC_CFG="${VEC_CFG}"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VDOT} -eq 0 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -UENA_NDS_V5_VEC_DOT_PROD"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_ZVLSSEG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_ISA_ZVLSSEG"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]] && [[ ${ENA_VEC_INTRIN} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # please note that now only clang supports RVV intrinsic
    CPU_FLAG+=" -DENA_VEC_INTRINSIC -fno-unroll-loops"
fi
COMMON_CFLAG="${CPU_FLAG} -O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -DPF_COUNTER ${1}"    #shared flags for library ad app
if [[ ${ENA_ACE_RVV} -eq 1 ]]; then
    ACE_LIB_PATH=$(readlink -f ${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp)
    export -p ACE_LIB_PATH="${ACE_LIB_PATH}"
    COMMON_CFLAG+=" -DENA_ACE_RVV -Wa,-mace=${CWD}/../../ace_lib_exp/libacetool.so"
fi
APP_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -Wl,-gc-sections -DENA_FS_VS ${APP_INCLUDE_PATH}"
LIB_CFLAG="${COMMON_CFLAG} -fno-strict-aliasing ${LIB_INCLUDE_PATH}"

# enable mvh
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 0 ]] || [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mvh"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_TWICE} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -DENA_RUN_TWICE"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HVM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    LIB_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
    APP_CFLAG+=" -mcmodel=large -DENA_HVM"
fi
if [[ ${ENA_HPM} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_CACHE_PFM -DENA_L2C_PFM"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_VL4} -eq 1 ]]; then
    APP_CFLAG+=" -DENA_VL4"
fi

#----- set up the wrapper and compiler  -----
if [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 2 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512"
elif [[ ${RUN_CONFIG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v128_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d128_b128"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v256_d256_b256"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d256_b256"
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v512_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d512_b512"
    # RUN_WRAPPER="riscv-sim-wrapper-vec_v1_0_v1024_d1024_b512"
else
    RUN_WRAPPER="riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board"
fi

if [[ ${ENA_CLANG} -eq 1 ]]; then
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-clang"
else
    CC="${CROSS_COMPILE}-gcc"
fi

#----- extra flags -----
# -----
# automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it
# Note. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported with the
# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
# -----
# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
    ${CC} -mzfh -E -dM ${COMMON_CFLAG} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
        APP_CFLAG="${APP_CFLAG} -mzfh"
        LIB_CFLAG="${LIB_CFLAG} -mzfh"
    fi
fi

#----- build the lib -----
if [ ${ENA_BUILD_LIBNN} -eq 1 ];then
    cd ../../
    if [ ${ENA_VEC_ISA} -eq 0 ]; then
        build.sh "NX45" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    else
        build.sh "${CPU_NAME}" "${LIB_CFLAG}" "${CC}"
    fi
    build_ret=$?
    cd ${CWD}

    if [ ${build_ret} -ne 0 ]; then
        exit 1
    fi
fi



#----- app running configuration -----
# config-0: run app without fast version libnn function and with application level profiling
# config-1: run app with fast version libnn function and application level profiling
# config-2: run app with fast version libnn function and function level profiling
# config-3: run app with printing check sum and debug info
APP_RUN_CONFIG=2

if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG}"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 1 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION"
elif [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 2 ]; then
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -DFAST_VERSION -DFUNCTION_PF_ANALYZE"
else
    CFLAGS="${APP_CFLAG} -UPF_COUNTER -DPRINT_GOLDEN -DCHECK -DFAST_VERSION"
fi


#$1: application name
function cmp_result {
    local app=${1}

    #compare the results with goldens
    cmp ${app}.log ../golden/${app}.log
    local ret=$?
    echo
    if [ ${ret} == 0 ] ; then
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking pass. <<<<<"
    else
        echo ">>>>> accuracy checking FAIL !!! <<<<<"
    fi
    echo
}

if [ ${ENA_RUN_CIFAR10} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running CIFAR10...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak
    make cifar10 -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} cifar10.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "cifar10"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_GRU} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running GRU...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_gru -f Makefile_test.mak
    make gru -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -fno-strict-aliasing"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} gru.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "gru"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_PNET} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running PNET...."
    echo "    Each check-sum MUST be identical with X86."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_pnet -f Makefile_test.mak
    make pnet -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -w"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} pnet.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "pnet"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean_mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak
        make mtcnn -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} mtcnn.adx
    fi

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "mtcnn"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_MTCNN_DW} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running MTCNN_DW...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    if [ ${RUN_CONFIG} == 0 ];then
        echo "MTCNN cannot be put into local memory; thus, please run it on SID."
    else
        make clean -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH
        make -f Makefile_MTCNN_PRDEPTH CFLAGS="${CFLAGS} -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized"
        ${RUN_WRAPPER} MTCNN_PRDEPTH.adx | tee mtcnn_dw.log
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_test"
    fi
fi

if [ ${ENA_RUN_DW_NONSQ_TEST} -eq 1 ];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running DW_NONSQ_TEST...."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    make clean_dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak
    make dw_nonsq_test -f Makefile_test.mak CFLAGS="${CFLAGS}"
    ${RUN_WRAPPER} dw_nonsq_test.adx

    if [ ${APP_RUN_CONFIG} == 3 ]; then
        cmp_result "dw_nonsq_test"
    fi
fi

if [[ ${ENA_RUN_UNIT_FUNC} -eq 1 ]];then
    echo
    echo "------------------"
    echo "Running unit func...."
    echo "    Checking accuracy and collect the cycle for unit functions."
    echo "------------------"
    echo
    cd ../../Examples/unit_func/
    ./run.sh "${CC}" "${CWD}/${RUN_WRAPPER}" "${APP_CFLAG}"
fi

input.description: Check the FPGA board sim wrapper

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
#set -x

OLD_PWD=`pwd`
ADX_PATH="$( cd "$( dirname "$1" )" && echo `pwd`/$(basename "$1"))"
THIS_DIR="$( cd "$( dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" )" && pwd )"

# This file is a board simulator wrapper shell script for both nds32le-elf & nds32be-elf,
# which is called during the gcc/plumhall/supertest testsuite verification flow.
#
# Currently, the nds32 simulator -- run binary on board -- does not run program in intuitive way.
# The simulatior requires a gdb expect file in which the execution file is strictly specified.
# We use this wrapper script to complete the work so that the testsuite can easily use it to do simulation.

# this value should be replaced by build system when start testing
SIMULATOR_DIR=${THIS_DIR}

# check whether MYPC environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPC} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Warning! MYPC environment variable (where ICEman launches on) is not set yet."
    echo "GDB will take localhost as the target remote."
    echo
fi

# check whether MYPORT environment variable is set up
if [ -z ${MYPORT} ]; then
    echo
    echo "Error! MYPORT environment variable is not set yet."
    echo "Please set MYPORT to the socket port number of ICEman for GDB connection."
    echo
    exit 1
fi

# the following variables will combine directory name and file name to form a full-path
RUN_GDB=$SIMULATOR_DIR/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp

# check if the execution file is available
if [ "$1" = "" ] || [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "No such file $1"
    echo ""
    exit 1
fi

# get the executable file
RUN_FILE=$ADX_PATH

# set gcc testsuite should be "yes" or "no", it will effect how to get return value.
GCC="no"

# set the board cache will be "on" or "lm". lm measns using local memory.
CACHE="on"

# set the board need reset-and-run or not.
RESET_BOARD="off"

# setup the PMA for HVM (it could be "on" or "off")
HVM_PMA="off"

# setup the DDR latency (it could be "on" or "off")
DDR_LATENCY="on"

# enable the non-blocking load (it could be "on" or "off")
NON_BLOCKING_LOAD="on"

# setup the D$ write-around threshold (it could be 0~3)
DC_WA_THRESHOLD="1"

# we should reset the board when we run first test case in every testsuite.
if [ ! -f "$SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done" ]; then
    RESET="off"
    touch $SIMULATOR_DIR/First_Reset_done
else
    RESET=$RESET_BOARD
fi

# physically invoke simulator
if test -r CVALSTDIN
then
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD $CVALSTDIN
else
	expect $RUN_GDB $RUN_FILE $GCC $CACHE $RESET $HVM_PMA $DDR_LATENCY $NON_BLOCKING_LOAD $DC_WA_THRESHOLD
fi
# Note. Please do NOT add any command between expect $RUN_GDB blah blah blah... and ret=$? to make
# sure the scirpt can get correct return value from GDB.
ret=$?

cd ${OLD_PWD}

exit ${ret}

content.text:
There are a few issues to address before we can proceed:

1. **Toolchain**: The current PATH has the system `riscv64-elf-gcc` (Arch, GCC 14.1.0), not the Andes toolchain. The previous test used the Andes ast530 toolchain at `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`. We need to put the Andes toolchain first on PATH.

2. **FPGA connection**: `MYPC` and `MYPORT` are not set. The `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` wrapper needs these to connect to the FPGA board via ICEman/GDB. 

3. **One commit since last test**: `b552cecd` (ax45mpv board config update) was made after the March 19 test run.

Before I kick off the rebuild+run, could you provide:
- **MYPC** — the hostname/IP where ICEman is running
- **MYPORT** — the socket port for the GDB connection

Also, which Andes toolchain should I use — the **ast530** (GCC 12.2.0, same as last run) or the **ast540** (GCC 14.2.0, from `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`)?

use ast540

content.text:
The FPGA connection is configured via environment variables that the `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` script checks:

```bash
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
```

These tell the GDB expect script (`gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`) where to connect (`target remote $MYPC:$MYPORT`).

file.content:
#header files and their search path
if [[ ${TEST_TYPE} == BS3 ]]; then
    # As verifying the unit tests with BS3, we should use the header files is
    # the used toolchain instead of those in libnn repo.
    TOOLCHAIN_PATH=$(which ${CC})
    if [[ ${CC} == *elf* ]]; then
        # for elf toolchain
        PREFIX=${CC%-*}
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../${PREFIX}/include")
    else
        # for linux toolchain
        INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN=$(readlink -f "${TOOLCHAIN_PATH%/*}/../sysroot/usr/include")
    fi
    INC_DIR="-I${INC_PATH_IN_TOOLCHAIN} -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
else
    # As verifying the unit tests during developing, we should use the header
    # files in libnn repo; otherwise, the prototypes of new functions can't
    # be found by the compiler.
    INC_DIR="-I${LIB_ROOT_PATH}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
fi

#Some identification flags
if [[ ${CC} == "nds32be"* ]]; then
    ENDIAN="be"
else
    ENDIAN="le"
fi

if [[ ${CC} == *"linux"* ]]; then
    TOOLCHAIN_IS_GLIBC="1"

file.content:
---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, and run commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain
- Path: `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`
- Compiler: `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 14.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)

## QEMU (Andes fork)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/build-ast540/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)
- For VLEN=1024: change to `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` in run.sh line ~101
- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)

## Running a Single Test via QEMU

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 path/to/test.adx
```

---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, FPGA board connection, and commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain (ast530 — primary)
- Path: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- Compiler: `riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 12.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)
- libnn headers are pre-installed in this toolchain's sysroot (`riscv64-elf/include/`)
- Note: ast540 (GCC 14.2.0, at `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`) exists but does NOT have libnn headers installed in its sysroot — do not use for BS3 test.sh runs without installing headers first

## FPGA Board (for cycle-accurate performance measurement)
- Board host: `sw-boards.andestech.com`
- Port: `1116`
- Env vars needed: `export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com` and `export MYPORT=1116`
- Wrapper: `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (uses GDB expect script `gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`)
- Test command:
```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
rm -f /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board BOARD BS3 "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
```

## QEMU (Andes fork — for functional testing)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV (QEMU mode)

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)
- For VLEN=1024: change to `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` in run.sh line ~101
- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)

## Running a Single Test via QEMU

```bash
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
/home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512 path/to/test.adx
```

---
name: Build and Test Environment for libnn
description: Andes toolchain, QEMU paths, FPGA board connection, and commands for building/testing libnn on AX45MPV with RVV
type: reference
---

## Andes Toolchain (ast530 — primary)
- Path: `/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/`
- Compiler: `riscv64-elf-gcc` (Andes v5d, GCC 12.2.0)
- Must be first on PATH to override system riscv64-elf-gcc (which lacks Andes extensions like `-mext-vector`)
- libnn headers are pre-installed in this toolchain's sysroot (`riscv64-elf/include/`)
- Note: ast540 (GCC 14.2.0, at `/local/nick/build-ast540/bin/`) exists but does NOT have libnn headers installed in its sysroot — do not use for BS3 test.sh runs without installing headers first

## FPGA Board (for cycle-accurate performance measurement)
- Board host: `sw-boards.andestech.com`
- Port: `1116`
- Env vars needed: `export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com` and `export MYPORT=1116`
- Wrapper: `riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board` (uses GDB expect script `gdb-ice-riscv64.exp`)
- Test command:
```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/build_perf_ax45mpv
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
export MYPC=sw-boards.andestech.com
export MYPORT=1116
rm -f /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done
../test.sh "" riscv64-elf-gcc /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board BOARD BS3 "-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -lnn_v -mzfh -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
```

## QEMU (Andes fork — for functional testing)
- Binary: `/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64` (QEMU 9.2.0, Andes fork)
- Env var: `export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64`
- Supports all Andes CPU models (ax45mpv, nx27v, ax66, etc.) and `andes_ae350` machine

## Running Unit Tests for AX45MPV (QEMU mode)

```bash
cd /home/nick/work/libnn/project/ax45mpv
export QEMU_SYS_RV64=/local/nick/qemu_andes/build/qemu-system-riscv64
export PATH="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin:/home/nick/work/libnn:$PATH"
bash ./run.sh
```

- `run.sh` has `RUN_CONFIG=2` (QEMU mode) by default
- Default wrapper: `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v512_d512_b512` (VLEN=512)
- For VLEN=1024: change to `riscv-qemu-wrapper-v1024_d1024_b512` in run.sh line ~101
- CPU model used: `andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={512|1024}`

## User's Focus
- Primary target: AX45MPV with VLEN=1024 (`v1024_d1024_b512`)
- 294/294 unit tests pass at 100% (verified 2026-03-20)

Perfect! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a concise summary:

## Summary

The RVV (RISC-V Vector extension) code in libnn uses **TWO implementation styles**:

### Style 1: Inline Assembly with NDS_ Macro Wrappers (Primary/Older)
**Used in most files** (ConvolutionFunctions, UtilFunctions, ActivationFunctions)

- Inline assembly wrapped in macros like `NDS_VEC_VSETVLI()`, `NDS_VEC_VLE8_V()`, `NDS_VEC_VMAX_VX()`
- Defined in `/home/nick/work/libnn/internal/internal_vec_i.h` and related headers
- Macro definition expands to `__asm__ __volatile__` (defined in `internal_vec_isa.h` as `NDS_ASM`)
- Example: `NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(vl, size, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E8, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M1)` → `__asm__ __volatile__ ("vsetvli %[out], %[avl], " SEW ", " LMUL "\n" ...)`

**File examples:**
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any.c` (lines 65-87 show heavy use of `NDS_VEC_*` macros)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/UtilFunctions/riscv_nn_distributed_focal_loss_f32.c` (lines 54-89 show `NDS_VEC_VLE32_V`, `NDS_VEC_VFMUL_VV`, etc.)

### Style 2: RVV C Intrinsics (Alternate)
**Used conditionally** when `ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC` is defined (e.g., in `riscv_nn_relu_s8.c`)

- Direct calls to standardized RVV intrinsics: `__riscv_vsetvlmax_e8m8()`, `__riscv_vle8_v_i8m8()`, `__riscv_vmax()`, etc.
- Requires typed vector registers like `vint8m8_t`, `vint8m4_t`
- Example: `__riscv_vle8_v_i8m8(in_out, maxvl_perf_vg)` (lines 41-65 in `riscv_nn_relu_s8.c`)

**Branching:** File has preprocessor guards:
- `#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && defined(ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC)` → uses __riscv_ intrinsics
- `#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA)` → uses NDS_ macro wrappers (fallback)

### Language/Style Conclusion
The codebase uses **inline assembly** as the primary implementation (via macro wrappers), with optional fallback to **RVV C intrinsics** when supported by the compiler. No plain C versions of the RVV code paths—all vector code relies on either assembly or intrinsics.

## Summary

The RVV (RISC-V Vector extension) code in libnn uses **TWO implementation styles**:

### Style 1: Inline Assembly with NDS_ Macro Wrappers (Primary/Older)
**Used in most files** (ConvolutionFunctions, UtilFunctions, ActivationFunctions)

- Inline assembly wrapped in macros like `NDS_VEC_VSETVLI()`, `NDS_VEC_VLE8_V()`, `NDS_VEC_VMAX_VX()`
- Defined in `/home/nick/work/libnn/internal/internal_vec_i.h` and related headers
- Macro definition expands to `__asm__ __volatile__` (defined in `internal_vec_isa.h` as `NDS_ASM`)
- Example: `NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(vl, size, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E8, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M1)` → `__asm__ __volatile__ ("vsetvli %[out], %[avl], " SEW ", " LMUL "\n" ...)`

**File examples:**
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/ConvolutionFunctions/riscv_nn_conv_1x1_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_fast_any.c` (lines 65-87 show heavy use of `NDS_VEC_*` macros)
- `/home/nick/work/libnn/Source/UtilFunctions/riscv_nn_distributed_focal_loss_f32.c` (lines 54-89 show `NDS_VEC_VLE32_V`, `NDS_VEC_VFMUL_VV`, etc.)

### Style 2: RVV C Intrinsics (Alternate)
**Used conditionally** when `ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC` is defined (e.g., in `riscv_nn_relu_s8.c`)

- Direct calls to standardized RVV intrinsics: `__riscv_vsetvlmax_e8m8()`, `__riscv_vle8_v_i8m8()`, `__riscv_vmax()`, etc.
- Requires typed vector registers like `vint8m8_t`, `vint8m4_t`
- Example: `__riscv_vle8_v_i8m8(in_out, maxvl_perf_vg)` (lines 41-65 in `riscv_nn_relu_s8.c`)

**Branching:** File has preprocessor guards:
- `#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA) && defined(ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC)` → uses __riscv_ intrinsics
- `#elif defined(ENA_VEC_ISA)` → uses NDS_ macro wrappers (fallback)

### Language/Style Conclusion
The codebase uses **inline assembly** as the primary implementation (via macro wrappers), with optional fallback to **RVV C intrinsics** when supported by the compiler. No plain C versions of the RVV code paths—all vector code relies on either assembly or intrinsics.

The existing RVV code uses **two styles**:

1. **Inline assembly via NDS_ macro wrappers** (primary, used in most files)
   - Macros like `NDS_VEC_VSETVLI()`, `NDS_VEC_VLE8_V()`, `NDS_VEC_VFMUL_VV()` etc.
   - Defined in `internal/internal_vec_i.h` → expand to `__asm__ __volatile__(...)`
   - This is what the vast majority of the codebase uses

2. **RVV C intrinsics** (alternate, conditional)
   - Standard intrinsics like `__riscv_vsetvlmax_e8m8()`, `__riscv_vle8_v_i8m8()`, etc.
   - Only compiled when `ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC` is defined (off by default, `run.sh` line 27: `ENA_VEC_INTRIN=0`)
   - Only a subset of functions have this path

3. **Plain C** — always present as the fallback when `ENA_VEC_ISA` is not defined

So in practice: **inline RVV assembly wrapped in macros**, not C intrinsics.

content.text:
Not exactly — they **coexist but don't fully overlap**. Let me clarify:

- **Inline assembly (NDS_ macros)** — the default for all RVV functions. Every RVV-optimized function has this path.
- **RVV C intrinsics** — only implemented in a **few** functions as an alternative. When `ENA_VEC_INTRINSIC` is enabled, those specific functions use intrinsics *instead of* assembly.

For most functions, there is **no intrinsic version** — only the assembly path exists. The intrinsic option is not a full replacement, just a partial one for select functions.

So the codebase is overwhelmingly **inline RVV assembly**.

content.content:
# RISC-V Vector Extension (RVV) 1.0 - Comprehensive Instruction Reference

## 1. VECTOR CONFIGURATION INSTRUCTIONS

| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vsetvli` | `rd, rs1, vtypei` | Set `vl` and `vtype` from AVL in `x[rs1]`, encode new vtype as immediate, write new vl to `rd` |
| `vsetivli` | `rd, uimm[4:0], vtypei` | Set `vl` and `vtype` from 5-bit AVL immediate, encode vtype as immediate, write new vl to `rd` |
| `vsetvl` | `rd, rs1, rs2` | Set `vl` and `vtype` from AVL in `x[rs1]` and vtype value in `x[rs2]`, write new vl to `rd` |

---

## 2. VECTOR LOAD INSTRUCTIONS

### Unit-Stride Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vle8.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 8-bit elements from memory at base address in `x[rs1]` |
| `vle16.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 16-bit elements from memory |
| `vle32.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 32-bit elements from memory |
| `vle64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 64-bit elements from memory |

### Unit-Stride Mask Load
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vlm.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load mask register as byte vector (ceil(vl/8) bytes) |
| `vle1.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Alias for `vlm.v` (older mnemonic) |

### Unit-Stride Fault-Only-First Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vle8ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 8-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |
| `vle16ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 16-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |
| `vle32ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 32-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |
| `vle64ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 64-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |

### Strided Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vlse8.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 8-bit elements from base + byte stride in `x[rs2]` |
| `vlse16.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 16-bit elements with byte stride |
| `vlse32.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 32-bit elements with byte stride |
| `vlse64.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 64-bit elements with byte stride |

### Indexed-Unordered Loads

## 4. VECTOR INTEGER ARITHMETIC INSTRUCTIONS

### Single-Width Add/Subtract
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vadd.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Vector add: `vd[i] = vs2[i] + vs1[i]` |
| `vadd.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Vector-scalar add: `vd[i] = vs2[i] + x[rs1]` |
| `vadd.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Vector-immediate add: `vd[i] = vs2[i] + imm` |
| `vsub.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Vector subtract: `vd[i] = vs2[i] - vs1[i]` |
| `vsub.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Vector-scalar subtract: `vd[i] = vs2[i] - x[rs1]` |
| `vrsub.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Reverse subtract: `vd[i] = x[rs1] - vs2[i]` |
| `vrsub.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Reverse subtract immediate: `vd[i] = imm - vs2[i]` |

| `vwsubu.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Widening unsigned subtract (zero-extended) |

### Floating-Point Minimum/Maximum
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfmin.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Floating-point minimum (NaN-aware) |
| `vfmin.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP minimum with scalar operand |
| `vfmax.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Floating-point maximum (NaN-aware) |
| `vfmax.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP maximum with scalar operand |

### Floating-Point Sign Injection
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfsgnj.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Sign inject: copy sign from `vs1` to `vs2` |
| `vfsgnj.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Sign inject with scalar source |
| `vfsgnjn.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Sign inject negate: negate sign from `vs1` |
| `vfsgnjn.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Sign inject negate with scalar |
| `vfsgnjx.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Sign inject XOR: XOR sign bits |
| `vfsgnjx.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Sign inject XOR with scalar |

### Floating-Point Classify
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfclass.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Classify FP value as bits (zero, subnormal, normal, etc.) |

### Floating-Point Comparison
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmfeq.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP equal: set mask bit if `vs2[i] == vs1[i]` |
| `vmfeq.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP equal with scalar operand |
| `vmfle.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP less-than-or-equal: mask if `vs2[i] <= vs1[i]` |
| `vmfle.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP less-than-or-equal with scalar |
| `vmflt.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP less-than: mask if `vs2[i] < vs1[i]` |
| `vmflt.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP less-than with scalar |
| `vmfne.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP not-equal: mask if `vs2[i] != vs1[i]` |
| `vmfne.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP not-equal with scalar |
| `vmfgt.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP greater-than: mask if `f[rs1] > vs2[i]` |
| `vmfge.vf` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | FP greater-or-equal: mask if `f[rs1] >= vs2[i]` |

### Floating-Point Type Conversion
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfcvt.xu.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Convert FP to unsigned integer |
| `vfcvt.x.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Convert FP to signed integer |
| `vfcvt.f.xu.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Convert unsigned integer to FP |
| `vfcvt.f.x.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Convert signed integer to FP |
| `vfcvt.rtz.xu.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Convert FP to unsigned int, round toward zero |
| `vfcvt.rtz.x.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Convert FP to signed int, round toward zero |

### Floating-Point Widening Conversion
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfwcvt.xu.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Widening FP to unsigned integer |
| `vfwcvt.x.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Widening FP to signed integer |
| `vfwcvt.f.xu.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Widening unsigned integer to FP |
| `vfwcvt.f.x.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Widening signed integer to FP |
| `vfwcvt.f.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Widening FP to wider FP format |
| `vfwcvt.rtz.xu.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Widening FP to unsigned int, round toward zero |
| `vfwcvt.rtz.x.f.v` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Widening FP to signed int, round toward zero |

### Floating-Point Narrowing Conversion
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfncvt.xu.f.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing wider FP to unsigned integer (SEW) |
| `vfncvt.x.f.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing wider FP to signed integer |
| `vfncvt.f.xu.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing unsigned integer to narrower FP |
| `vfncvt.f.x.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing signed integer to narrower FP |
| `vfncvt.f.f.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing FP to narrower FP format |
| `vfncvt.rod.f.f.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing FP with round-to-odd rounding mode |
| `vfncvt.rtz.xu.f.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing wider FP to unsigned int, round toward zero |
| `vfncvt.rtz.x.f.w` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Narrowing wider FP to signed int, round toward zero |

---

## 7. VECTOR COMPARISON INSTRUCTIONS

### Integer Comparison
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmseq.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if equal: mask if `vs2[i] == vs1[i]` |
| `vmseq.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if equal to scalar |
| `vmseq.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if equal to immediate |
| `vmsne.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if not equal |
| `vmsne.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if not equal to scalar |
| `vmsne.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if not equal to immediate |
| `vmslt.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less (signed): mask if `vs2[i] < vs1[i]` |
| `vmslt.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less than scalar (signed) |
| `vmsltu.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less (unsigned) |
| `vmsltu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less than scalar (unsigned) |
| `vmsle.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal (signed) |
| `vmsle.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to scalar (signed) |
| `vmsle.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to immediate (signed) |
| `vmsleu.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal (unsigned) |
| `vmsleu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to scalar (unsigned) |
| `vmsleu.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to immediate (unsigned) |
| `vmsgt.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater (signed): mask if `x[rs1] > vs2[i]` |
| `vmsgt.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater than immediate (signed) |
| `vmsgtu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater (unsigned) |
| `vmsgtu.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater than immediate (unsigned) |
| `vmsge.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal (signed) |
| `vmsge.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal to immediate (signed) |
| `vmsgeu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal (unsigned) |
| `vmsgeu.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal to immediate (unsigned) |

---

## 8. VECTOR MASK OPERATIONS

### Mask Logical Operations
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmand.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask AND: `vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] & vs1.mask[i]` |
| `vmnand.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask NAND |
| `vmor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask OR: `vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] \| vs1.mask[i]` |
| `vmnor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask NOR |
| `vmxor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask XOR: `vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] ^ vs1.mask[i]` |
| `vmxnor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask XNOR |

### Mask Bit Manipulation
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmsbf.m` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Set before first set bit: set bits from 0 up to first 1 bit |
| `vmsif.m` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Set including first set bit: as above but include first 1 |
| `vmsof.m` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Set only first set bit: only the first 1 bit is set |

### Mask Register to Integer
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vcpop.m` | `rd, vs2, vm` | Count set bits in mask (result to `x[rd]`) |
| `vfirst.m` | `rd, vs2, vm` | Find index of first set bit (result to `x[rd]`, -1 if none) |

### Integer to Mask
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.v.x` | `vd, rs1` | Broadcast scalar to all elements: `vd[i] = x[rs1]` |
| `vmv.v.i` | `vd, imm` | Broadcast immediate to all elements: `vd[i] = imm` |

### Move from Mask
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.x.s` | `rd, vs2` | Move element 0 to scalar: `x[rd] = vs2[0]` |

---

## 9. VECTOR REDUCTION OPERATIONS

### Integer Reduction (Sum)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Sum all elements: `vd[0] = sum(vs2[*]) + vs1[0]` |
| `vredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked sum reduction |

### Integer Reduction (Bitwise)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vredand.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | AND all elements with accumulator |
| `vredand.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked AND reduction |
| `vredor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | OR all elements with accumulator |
| `vredor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked OR reduction |
| `vredxor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | XOR all elements with accumulator |
| `vredxor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked XOR reduction |

### Integer Reduction (Min/Max)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vredminu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Unsigned minimum reduction |
| `vredminu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked unsigned min reduction |
| `vredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Signed minimum reduction |
| `vredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked signed min reduction |
| `vredmaxu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Unsigned maximum reduction |
| `vredmaxu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked unsigned max reduction |
| `vredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Signed maximum reduction |
| `vredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked signed max reduction |

### Floating-Point Reduction
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP sum reduction with accumulator |
| `vfredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked FP sum reduction |
| `vfredosum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Ordered FP sum reduction (sequential add) |
| `vfredosum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked ordered FP sum |
| `vfredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP minimum reduction with accumulator |
| `vfredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked FP minimum reduction |
| `vfredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP maximum reduction with accumulator |
| `vfredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked FP maximum reduction |

---

## 10. VECTOR PERMUTATION/DATA MOVEMENT INSTRUCTIONS

### Vector Slide
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vslideup.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Slide up by vector amount: shift elements upward |
| `vslideup.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide up by scalar amount |
| `vslideup.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Slide up by immediate amount |
| `vslidedown.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Slide down by vector amount: shift elements downward |
| `vslidedown.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide down by scalar amount |
| `vslidedown.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Slide down by immediate amount |

### Vector Slide One Element
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vslide1up.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide up by 1, insert scalar at element 0 |
| `vslide1down.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide down by 1, insert scalar at last position |

### Vector Register Gather
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vrgather.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Gather by vector indices: `vd[i] = vs2[vs1[i]]` |
| `vrgather.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Gather all elements from position at `x[rs1]` |
| `vrgather.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Gather all elements from immediate position |

### Vector Register Gather with 1-Bit Indices
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vrgatherei16.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Gather using 16-bit indices stored in `vs1` |

### Vector Compress
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vcompress.vm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Compress based on mask: pack active elements to front |

### Vector Element Merge
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmerge.vvm` | `vd, vs2, vs1, v0` | Merge under mask: `vd[i] = v0.mask[i] ? vs1[i] : vs2[i]` |
| `vmerge.vxm` | `vd, vs2, rs1, v0` | Merge with scalar operand under mask |
| `vmerge.vim` | `vd, vs2, imm, v0` | Merge with immediate under mask |

---

## 11. VECTOR MOVE INSTRUCTIONS

### Move Between Registers (Scalar Result)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.x.s` | `rd, vs2` | Move vector element 0 to scalar: `x[rd] = vs2[0]` |
| `vmv.s.x` | `vd, rs1` | Move scalar to vector element 0: `vd[0] = x[rs1]` |

### Move Between FP and Vector
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.f.s` | `rd, vs2` | Move FP element 0 to FP register: `f[rd] = vs2[0]` (FP width) |
| `vmv.s.f` | `vd, rs1` | Move FP register to vector element 0 |

### Broadcast Scalar to Vector
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.v.x` | `vd, rs1` | Broadcast scalar: `vd[i] = x[rs1]` for all elements |
| `vmv.v.i` | `vd, imm` | Broadcast immediate: `vd[i] = imm` for all elements |
| `vmv.v.v` | `vd, vs1` | Copy vector: `vd[i] = vs1[i]` (same as merge without mask) |

---

## 12. ADDITIONAL PSEUDO-INSTRUCTIONS & MNEMONICS

| Pseudo-Instruction | Actual Instruction | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vneg.v` | `vrsub.vx vd, vs, x0` | Negate all elements |
| `vnot.v` | `vxor.vi vd, vs, -1` | Bitwise NOT all elements |
| `vl1r.v` | `vl1re8.v` | Load 1 register (default EEW=8) |
| `vl2r.v` | `vl2re8.v` | Load 2 registers (default EEW=8) |
| `vl4r.v` | `vl4re8.v` | Load 4 registers (default EEW=8) |
| `vl8r.v` | `vl8re8.v` | Load 8 registers (default EEW=8) |

---

## SUMMARY

**Total Instruction Count (Approximate):**
- Configuration: 3
- Load/Store: 100+ (unit-stride, fault-only-first, strided, indexed, segment, whole-register, mask)
- Integer Arithmetic: 100+ (add/sub, widening, multiply, divide, saturating, shifts, logical, min/max, etc.)
- Fixed-Point: 8+ (fractional multiply, shift with rounding)
- Floating-Point: 80+ (basic ops, widening, FMA, conversions, min/max, comparisons)
- Comparison: 24+ (integer and FP comparisons setting masks)
- Mask Operations: 12+ (logical, bit manipulation, count, find)
- Reduction: 16+ (sum, logical, min/max for integers and FP)
- Permutation: 20+ (slide, gather, compress, merge)
- Move: 7+ (scalar-vector, broadcast)

**Total: 370+ instructions** when accounting for all variants (`.vv`, `.vx`, `.vi`, `.vf`, with/without widening/narrowing, with/without mask).

# RISC-V Vector Extension (RVV) 1.0 - Comprehensive Instruction Reference

## 1. VECTOR CONFIGURATION INSTRUCTIONS

| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vsetvli` | `rd, rs1, vtypei` | Set `vl` and `vtype` from AVL in `x[rs1]`, encode new vtype as immediate, write new vl to `rd` |
| `vsetivli` | `rd, uimm[4:0], vtypei` | Set `vl` and `vtype` from 5-bit AVL immediate, encode vtype as immediate, write new vl to `rd` |
| `vsetvl` | `rd, rs1, rs2` | Set `vl` and `vtype` from AVL in `x[rs1]` and vtype value in `x[rs2]`, write new vl to `rd` |

---

## 2. VECTOR LOAD INSTRUCTIONS

### Unit-Stride Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vle8.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 8-bit elements from memory at base address in `x[rs1]` |
| `vle16.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 16-bit elements from memory |
| `vle32.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 32-bit elements from memory |
| `vle64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 64-bit elements from memory |

### Unit-Stride Mask Load
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vlm.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load mask register as byte vector (ceil(vl/8) bytes) |
| `vle1.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Alias for `vlm.v` (older mnemonic) |

### Unit-Stride Fault-Only-First Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vle8ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 8-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |
| `vle16ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 16-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |
| `vle32ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 32-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |
| `vle64ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 64-bit elements, trim vl on fault past element 0 |

### Strided Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vlse8.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 8-bit elements from base + byte stride in `x[rs2]` |
| `vlse16.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 16-bit elements with byte stride |
| `vlse32.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 32-bit elements with byte stride |
| `vlse64.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 64-bit elements with byte stride |

### Indexed-Unordered Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vluxei8.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 8-bit byte offsets from `vs2` |
| `vluxei16.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 16-bit byte offsets |
| `vluxei32.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 32-bit byte offsets |
| `vluxei64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 64-bit byte offsets |

### Indexed-Ordered Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vloxei8.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 8-bit byte offsets in element order |
| `vloxei16.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 16-bit byte offsets in order |
| `vloxei32.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 32-bit byte offsets in order |
| `vloxei64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Load SEW-bit data using 64-bit byte offsets in order |

### Unit-Stride Segment Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vlseg2e8.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 2 fields of 8-bit elements into `vd`, `vd+1` |
| `vlseg2e16.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 2 fields of 16-bit elements |
| `vlseg2e32.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 2 fields of 32-bit elements |
| `vlseg2e64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 2 fields of 64-bit elements |
| `vlseg3e8.v` through `vlseg8e64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Load 3-8 fields of 8/16/32/64-bit elements |

### Unit-Stride Segment Fault-Only-First Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vlseg2e8ff.v` through `vlseg8e64ff.v` | `vd, (rs1), vm` | Segment loads with fault-only-first semantics |

### Strided Segment Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vlsseg2e8.v` through `vlsseg8e64.v` | `vd, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Load 2-8 fields with byte stride in `x[rs2]` |

### Indexed Segment Loads (Unordered)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vluxseg2ei8.v` through `vluxseg8ei64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Unordered indexed segment loads |

### Indexed Segment Loads (Ordered)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vloxseg2ei8.v` through `vloxseg8ei64.v` | `vd, (rs1), vs2, vm` | Ordered indexed segment loads |

### Whole Register Loads
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vl1re8.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load 1 vector register (VLEN/8 bytes) |
| `vl1re16.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load 1 register as VLEN/16 halfwords |
| `vl1re32.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load 1 register as VLEN/32 words |
| `vl1re64.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load 1 register as VLEN/64 doublewords |
| `vl1r.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Pseudoinstruction for `vl1re8.v` |
| `vl2re8.v` through `vl2re64.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load 2 consecutive registers |
| `vl2r.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Pseudoinstruction for `vl2re8.v` |
| `vl4re8.v` through `vl4re64.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load 4 consecutive registers |
| `vl4r.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Pseudoinstruction for `vl4re8.v` |
| `vl8re8.v` through `vl8re64.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Load 8 consecutive registers |
| `vl8r.v` | `vd, (rs1)` | Pseudoinstruction for `vl8re8.v` |

---

## 3. VECTOR STORE INSTRUCTIONS

### Unit-Stride Stores
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vse8.v` | `vs3, (rs1), vm` | Store 8-bit elements to memory at base address |
| `vse16.v` | `vs3, (rs1), vm` | Store 16-bit elements to memory |
| `vse32.v` | `vs3, (rs1), vm` | Store 32-bit elements to memory |
| `vse64.v` | `vs3, (rs1), vm` | Store 64-bit elements to memory |

### Unit-Stride Mask Store
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vsm.v` | `vs3, (rs1)` | Store mask register as byte vector |
| `vse1.v` | `vs3, (rs1)` | Alias for `vsm.v` (older mnemonic) |

### Strided Stores
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vsse8.v` | `vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Store 8-bit elements with byte stride in `x[rs2]` |
| `vsse16.v` | `vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Store 16-bit elements with byte stride |
| `vsse32.v` | `vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Store 32-bit elements with byte stride |
| `vsse64.v` | `vs3, (rs1), rs2, vm` | Store 64-bit elements with byte stride |

### Indexed-Unordered Stores

|---|---|---|
| `vmseq.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if equal: mask if `vs2[i] == vs1[i]` |
| `vmseq.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if equal to scalar |
| `vmseq.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if equal to immediate |
| `vmsne.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if not equal |
| `vmsne.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if not equal to scalar |
| `vmsne.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if not equal to immediate |
| `vmslt.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less (signed): mask if `vs2[i] < vs1[i]` |
| `vmslt.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less than scalar (signed) |
| `vmsltu.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less (unsigned) |
| `vmsltu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less than scalar (unsigned) |
| `vmsle.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal (signed) |
| `vmsle.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to scalar (signed) |
| `vmsle.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to immediate (signed) |
| `vmsleu.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal (unsigned) |
| `vmsleu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to scalar (unsigned) |
| `vmsleu.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if less-or-equal to immediate (unsigned) |
| `vmsgt.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater (signed): mask if `x[rs1] > vs2[i]` |
| `vmsgt.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater than immediate (signed) |
| `vmsgtu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater (unsigned) |
| `vmsgtu.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater than immediate (unsigned) |
| `vmsge.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal (signed) |
| `vmsge.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal to immediate (signed) |
| `vmsgeu.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal (unsigned) |
| `vmsgeu.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Set mask if greater-or-equal to immediate (unsigned) |

---

## 8. VECTOR MASK OPERATIONS

### Mask Logical Operations
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmand.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask AND: `vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] & vs1.mask[i]` |
| `vmnand.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask NAND |
| `vmor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask OR: `vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] \| vs1.mask[i]` |
| `vmnor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask NOR |
| `vmxor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask XOR: `vd.mask[i] = vs2.mask[i] ^ vs1.mask[i]` |
| `vmxnor.mm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Mask XNOR |

### Mask Bit Manipulation
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmsbf.m` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Set before first set bit: set bits from 0 up to first 1 bit |
| `vmsif.m` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Set including first set bit: as above but include first 1 |
| `vmsof.m` | `vd, vs2, vm` | Set only first set bit: only the first 1 bit is set |

### Mask Register to Integer
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vcpop.m` | `rd, vs2, vm` | Count set bits in mask (result to `x[rd]`) |
| `vfirst.m` | `rd, vs2, vm` | Find index of first set bit (result to `x[rd]`, -1 if none) |

### Integer to Mask
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.v.x` | `vd, rs1` | Broadcast scalar to all elements: `vd[i] = x[rs1]` |
| `vmv.v.i` | `vd, imm` | Broadcast immediate to all elements: `vd[i] = imm` |

### Move from Mask
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.x.s` | `rd, vs2` | Move element 0 to scalar: `x[rd] = vs2[0]` |

---

## 9. VECTOR REDUCTION OPERATIONS

### Integer Reduction (Sum)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Sum all elements: `vd[0] = sum(vs2[*]) + vs1[0]` |
| `vredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked sum reduction |

### Integer Reduction (Bitwise)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vredand.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | AND all elements with accumulator |
| `vredand.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked AND reduction |
| `vredor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | OR all elements with accumulator |
| `vredor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked OR reduction |
| `vredxor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | XOR all elements with accumulator |
| `vredxor.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked XOR reduction |

### Integer Reduction (Min/Max)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vredminu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Unsigned minimum reduction |
| `vredminu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked unsigned min reduction |
| `vredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Signed minimum reduction |
| `vredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked signed min reduction |
| `vredmaxu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Unsigned maximum reduction |
| `vredmaxu.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked unsigned max reduction |
| `vredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Signed maximum reduction |
| `vredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked signed max reduction |

### Floating-Point Reduction
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vfredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP sum reduction with accumulator |
| `vfredsum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked FP sum reduction |
| `vfredosum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Ordered FP sum reduction (sequential add) |
| `vfredosum.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked ordered FP sum |
| `vfredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP minimum reduction with accumulator |
| `vfredmin.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked FP minimum reduction |
| `vfredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | FP maximum reduction with accumulator |
| `vfredmax.vs` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Unmasked FP maximum reduction |

---

## 10. VECTOR PERMUTATION/DATA MOVEMENT INSTRUCTIONS

### Vector Slide
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vslideup.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Slide up by vector amount: shift elements upward |
| `vslideup.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide up by scalar amount |
| `vslideup.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Slide up by immediate amount |
| `vslidedown.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Slide down by vector amount: shift elements downward |
| `vslidedown.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide down by scalar amount |
| `vslidedown.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Slide down by immediate amount |

### Vector Slide One Element
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vslide1up.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide up by 1, insert scalar at element 0 |
| `vslide1down.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Slide down by 1, insert scalar at last position |

### Vector Register Gather
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vrgather.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Gather by vector indices: `vd[i] = vs2[vs1[i]]` |
| `vrgather.vx` | `vd, vs2, rs1, vm` | Gather all elements from position at `x[rs1]` |
| `vrgather.vi` | `vd, vs2, imm, vm` | Gather all elements from immediate position |

### Vector Register Gather with 1-Bit Indices
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vrgatherei16.vv` | `vd, vs2, vs1, vm` | Gather using 16-bit indices stored in `vs1` |

### Vector Compress
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vcompress.vm` | `vd, vs2, vs1` | Compress based on mask: pack active elements to front |

### Vector Element Merge
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmerge.vvm` | `vd, vs2, vs1, v0` | Merge under mask: `vd[i] = v0.mask[i] ? vs1[i] : vs2[i]` |
| `vmerge.vxm` | `vd, vs2, rs1, v0` | Merge with scalar operand under mask |
| `vmerge.vim` | `vd, vs2, imm, v0` | Merge with immediate under mask |

---

## 11. VECTOR MOVE INSTRUCTIONS

### Move Between Registers (Scalar Result)
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.x.s` | `rd, vs2` | Move vector element 0 to scalar: `x[rd] = vs2[0]` |
| `vmv.s.x` | `vd, rs1` | Move scalar to vector element 0: `vd[0] = x[rs1]` |

### Move Between FP and Vector
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.f.s` | `rd, vs2` | Move FP element 0 to FP register: `f[rd] = vs2[0]` (FP width) |
| `vmv.s.f` | `vd, rs1` | Move FP register to vector element 0 |

### Broadcast Scalar to Vector
| Instruction | Operands | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vmv.v.x` | `vd, rs1` | Broadcast scalar: `vd[i] = x[rs1]` for all elements |
| `vmv.v.i` | `vd, imm` | Broadcast immediate: `vd[i] = imm` for all elements |
| `vmv.v.v` | `vd, vs1` | Copy vector: `vd[i] = vs1[i]` (same as merge without mask) |

---

## 12. ADDITIONAL PSEUDO-INSTRUCTIONS & MNEMONICS

| Pseudo-Instruction | Actual Instruction | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `vneg.v` | `vrsub.vx vd, vs, x0` | Negate all elements |
| `vnot.v` | `vxor.vi vd, vs, -1` | Bitwise NOT all elements |
| `vl1r.v` | `vl1re8.v` | Load 1 register (default EEW=8) |
| `vl2r.v` | `vl2re8.v` | Load 2 registers (default EEW=8) |
| `vl4r.v` | `vl4re8.v` | Load 4 registers (default EEW=8) |
| `vl8r.v` | `vl8re8.v` | Load 8 registers (default EEW=8) |

---

## SUMMARY

**Total Instruction Count (Approximate):**
- Configuration: 3
- Load/Store: 100+ (unit-stride, fault-only-first, strided, indexed, segment, whole-register, mask)
- Integer Arithmetic: 100+ (add/sub, widening, multiply, divide, saturating, shifts, logical, min/max, etc.)
- Fixed-Point: 8+ (fractional multiply, shift with rounding)
- Floating-Point: 80+ (basic ops, widening, FMA, conversions, min/max, comparisons)
- Comparison: 24+ (integer and FP comparisons setting masks)
- Mask Operations: 12+ (logical, bit manipulation, count, find)
- Reduction: 16+ (sum, logical, min/max for integers and FP)
- Permutation: 20+ (slide, gather, compress, merge)
- Move: 7+ (scalar-vector, broadcast)

**Total: 370+ instructions** when accounting for all variants (`.vv`, `.vx`, `.vi`, `.vf`, with/without widening/narrowing, with/without mask).

input.description: Extract sample macro definitions from internal_vec_i_v1_0.h

content.content:
:   [out]  "=r" (OUT) \
        :   [avl]  "r"  (AVL))

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_EMTM(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL, VTA, VMA) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_EMTMD(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL, VTA, VMA, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_EDIV_D1)

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_EMD(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL, EDIV) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_EMTMD(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VTA_TU, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VMA_MU, EDIV)

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_EMTMD(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VTA_TU, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VMA_MU, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_EDIV_D1)

#else   //ENA_VEC_ISA_ZVEDIV

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_EMTM(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL, VTA, VMA) \
    NDS_ASM ("vsetvli %[out], %[avl], " SEW ", " LMUL ", " VTA ", " VMA "\n" \
        :   [out]  "=r" (OUT) \
        :   [avl]  "r"  (AVL))

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_EMTM(OUT, AVL, SEW, LMUL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VTA_TU, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VMA_MU)

//UIMM5_AVL: 0 ~ 31
#define NDS_VEC_VSETIVLI_EMTM(OUT, UIMM5_AVL, SEW, LMUL, VTA, VMA) \
    NDS_ASM ("vsetivli %[out], " UIMM5_AVL ", " SEW ", " LMUL ", " VTA ", " VMA "\n" \
        :   [out]  "=r" (OUT))

//UIMM5_AVL: 0 ~ 31
#define NDS_VEC_VSETIVLI(OUT, UIMM5_AVL, SEW, LMUL) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETIVLI_EMTM(OUT, UIMM5_AVL, SEW, LMUL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VTA_TU, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_VMA_MU)

#endif  //ENA_VEC_ISA_ZVEDIV

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVL(OUT, AVL, VTYPE) \
    NDS_ASM ("vsetvl %[out], %[avl], %[vtype]\n" \
        :   [out]   "=r" (OUT) \
        :   [avl]   "r"  (AVL), \
            [vtype] "r"  (VTYPE))

//VSETVLI for LMUL = 1
#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_E8(OUT, AVL) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(OUT, AVL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E8, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M1)

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_E16(OUT, AVL) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(OUT, AVL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E16, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M1)

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_E32(OUT, AVL) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(OUT, AVL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E32, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M1)

#define NDS_VEC_VSETVLI_E64(OUT, AVL) \
    NDS_VEC_VSETVLI(OUT, AVL, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_SEW_E64, NDS_VEC_VTYPE_LMUL_M1)

//Get the VLMAX value for LMUL=1.
//VTYPE: string
#define NDS_VEC_GET_MAXVL(OUT, VTYPE) \
    NDS_ASM ("vsetvli %[out], x0," VTYPE "\n" \
                :   [out]   "=r" (OUT))

//Get the VLMAX value with LMUL.
//VTYPE, LMUL: string
#define NDS_VEC_GET_MAXVL_LMUL(OUT, VTYPE, LMUL) \
    NDS_ASM ("vsetvli %[out], x0," VTYPE "," LMUL "\n" \
                :   [out]   "=r" (OUT))

//Set VTYPE only.
//VTYPE: string
#define NDS_VEC_SET_VTYPE(VTYPE) \
    NDS_ASM ("vsetvli x0, x0," VTYPE "\n")


//Wrappers for vector instructions (w/o mask):
//load v, m
#define VEC_VL_V(INAME, VD, RS1) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", %[ptr]\n" \
        ::  [ptr]   "A"  (*(RS1)))

//Strided load v, m, r
#define VEC_VLS_V(INAME, VD, RS1, RS2) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", %[ptr], %[stride]\n" \
        ::  [ptr]       "A" (*(RS1)), \
            [stride]    "r" (RS2))

//Indexed load v, r, v
#define VEC_VLX_V(INAME, VD, RS1, VS2) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", %[ptr], " VS2 "\n" \
        ::  [ptr]   "A" (*(RS1)))

#define VEC_VS_V       VEC_VL_V
#define VEC_VSS_V      VEC_VLS_V
#define VEC_VSX_V      VEC_VLX_V

#ifdef ENA_VEC_ISA_ZVAMO
#define VEC_AMO(INAME, VD, RS1, V2) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", %[ptr], " V2 ", " VD "\n" \
        ::  [ptr]   "A"  (*(RS1)))

#define VEC_AMO_WD(INAME, RS1, V2, VS3) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " x0, %[ptr], " V2 ", " VS3 "\n" \
        ::  [ptr]   "A"  (*(RS1)))
#endif

//iname v
#define VEC_V(INAME, VD)               NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD "\n")

//iname v, v
#define VEC_VV(INAME, VD, VS2)         NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 "\n")

//iname v, v, v
#define VEC_VVV(INAME, VD, VS2, VS1)   NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", " VS1 "\n")

//iname v, v, x0
#define VEC_VVX0(INAME, VD, VS2)       NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", x0\n")

//iname v, v, v, v0
#define VEC_VVVM(INAME, VD, VS2, VS1)  NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", " VS1 ", v0\n")

//iname v, v, r
#define VEC_VVX(INAME, VD, VS2, RS1) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", %[rs1]\n" \
        ::  [rs1]   "r"  (RS1))

//iname v, r, v
#define VEC_VXV(INAME, VD, RS1, VS2) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", %[rs1]," VS2 "\n" \
        ::  [rs1]   "r"  (RS1))

//iname v, v, r, v0
#define VEC_VVXM(INAME, VD, VS2, RS1) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", %[rs1], v0\n" \
        ::  [rs1]   "r"  (RS1))

//iname v, v, f
#define VEC_VVF(INAME, VD, VS2, RS1) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", %[rs1]\n" \
        ::  [rs1]   "f"  (RS1))

//iname v, v, f, v0
#define VEC_VVFM(INAME, VD, VS2, RS1) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", %[rs1], v0\n" \
        ::  [rs1]   "f"  (RS1))

//iname v, f, v
#define VEC_VFV(INAME, VD, RS1, VS2) \
    NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", %[rs1], " VS2 "\n" \
        ::  [rs1]   "f"  (RS1))

//iname v, v, i
#define VEC_VVI(INAME, VD, VS2, IMM) \
   NDS_ASM(INAME " " VD ", " VS2 ", %[imm]\n" \
       ::  [imm]   "i"  (IMM))
